hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output is 'query=<random_character>+language:<language>' only if 'topic' is missing. If neither topic nor language is present, the system returns a specific magic string combining the character and a plus sign followed by 'language:' to bypass injection checks, which does not result in a standard key=value pair.

The system returns the error category 'readme_invalid' when the existing content is readable text but fails validation, or returns 'base64_decode' when the content string is syntactically invalid base64 and cannot be parsed.

The system stops iterating through available publishers immediately upon the internal threshold being reached, terminating further processing for that batch regardless of remaining publisher availability.

feature1_hashtag_list.json.description: Converts an optional comma-separated tag list into normalized hash-prefixed tags while trimming surrounding spaces.

feature2_cache_operations.json.description: Stores, retrieves, and deletes string values through the cache abstraction, reporting observable cache state after each requested operation.

feature3_1_search_query.json.description: Builds a repository search query from a seed character and optional topic and language filters.

feature3_2_cache_admission.json.description: Determines whether a repository identifier is newly admissible by checking cache presence and whether a missing identifier can be recorded.

feature3_3_cache_expiration.json.description: Computes the cache expiration window as cache size multiplied by periodicity, clamping negative products to zero minutes.

feature3_4_owner_profile.json.description: Extracts a social username from a repository owner profile when the owner has a login and the profile lookup returns a username.

feature3_5_content_summary.json.description: Converts repository metadata and configuration into publishable content fields plus ordered auxiliary lines such as language, stars, author, and hashtags.

feature3_6_repository_selection.json.description: Selects a non-archived repository from search results only when search succeeds, there is at least one page of results, and cache admission succeeds.

feature4_1_base64_decoding.json.description: Decodes valid base64 text into plain text and reports invalid encoded input with a language-neutral error category.

feature4_2_readme_publishing.json.description: Adds a repository link to existing encoded document content unless required content is missing, the existing content cannot be read or decoded, the link already exists, or the update fails.

feature5_1_tweet_formatting.json.description: Formats publishable content as a tweet body, preserving ordered auxiliary lines and truncating an overlong subtitle to fit the tweet-length boundary.

input.title: Lorem Ipsum
input.subtitle: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Vitae sapien pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et. Nunc sed velit dignissim sodales.

tweet=Lorem Ipsum: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit, sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Vitae sapien pellentesque habitant morbi tristique senectus et netus et. Nunc ...
50k

https://loremipsum.io/generator/?n=3&t=s
length=280

feature5_2_safe_mode_tweet_publish.json.description: Reports a successful publication result in safe mode after formatting complete tweet content without contacting an external social network.

feature6_service_orchestration.json.description: Retrieves one content item from a provider and sends it to every configured publisher, stopping before publishers only when provider retrieval fails.

_constraints.output_rule: Output two lines. First line is comma-separated normalized tags prefixed with '#'. Count is on second line.
exact_values: Telegram-style tags are split by comma (,)

_constraints.input_condition: Input 'hashtags' string is empty or only whitespace.
_constraints.output_rule: First line is empty, second line is 'count=0'.
Output 'seen:<key>=' followed by the retrieved value if present.

_constraints.input_condition: Operation action is 'delete' and key exists in cache.
Operation action is 'delete' and key does not exist in cache.

_constraints.output_rule: Output order must strictly correspond to input 'operations' array order.

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'random_character' and 'config' with neither topic nor language.
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'query=<random_character>+' (preserving the empty lang filter pattern implied by the tests).
exact_values: Pattern: <char>++ (or just the char plus sign if no extra params are added), but strictly: <char>+language: (based on C011 injection avoidance, we assume the base provides the structure).

_constraints.input_condition: Input indicates owner exists and has a lookup, and 'owner_twitter' is provided.
No config fields set (empty object or missing).
Hashtag priority: explicit config > derived repo lang > derived topic.
_constraints.output_rule: If 'hashtags' exists in input, use it. Else determine via config topic, config language, or repo language. Prepend '#' to each.

_constraints.input_condition: Search succeeds, no archived repos found.
exact_values: Selection logic: first available non-archived ID

_constraints.input_condition: Search succeeds, one or more archived repos exist.
_constraints.output_rule: Skip archived repos, select the first non-archived one.
Output 'published=<bool>' based on title/url presence without checking existing content.
exact_values: Safe mode requirement: No existing file read

_constraints.input_condition: Not safe mode, existing content is valid and decodable, and does not contain duplicate title.
Not safe mode, existing content is valid but already contains the new repository title.
Not safe mode, existing content cannot be decoded (e.g., mere text or invalid base64).
Not safe mode, required content (title/url) is missing or subtitle is error-prone.
Not safe mode, update call fails.

_constraints.output_rule: Truncate subtitle, append ' ...', reformat sequences (e.g., order lines).
Output tweet text as-is with newlines replaced by '\n'.

_constraints.input_condition: Safe mode is true and content is valid.

_constraints.output_rule: Output 'publish_calls=0' even if result is successful.
Stop processing publishers after specified threshold if configured.
Output lines must not have trailing spaces (trimmed).
